text: A thirty-year-old female patient was presented to our unit complaining of recurrent anal fistula. The patient underwent reconstructive proctocolectomy for familial adenomatosis polyposis in two thousand and one. In two thousand and seventeen, she underwent abscessotomy for poroproctitis. Later, the patient developed higher trans-sphincteric fistula. Legation of intra-sphincteric fistula tract was performed on April in two thousand and eighteen. Unfortunately, the fistula reoccurred in two weeks.
The patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position, and the surgeon identifies the internal and external openings of the anal fistula.

The surgeon performs thorough debridement of the fistula tract. A suture marks the internal opening, and a flexible cannula sheet is inserted into the external opening, extending to the internal opening for paste injection. The internal opening is then closed with multiple sutures.
The surgeon closes the external opening of the fistula with a single loose suture, completing the closure of the fistula tract.
